title: What’s the Fed’s next move?
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: The next Fed interest rate decision is scheduled for April 29, 2026. The Fed held rates steady at 3.5–3.75% in March. The Fed is likely to cut rates only if the
excerpt: The next Fed interest rate decision is scheduled for April 29, 2026. The Fed held rates steady at 3.5–3.75% in March. The Fed is likely to cut rates only if the labor market weakens significantly or if economic impacts from higher energy prices worsen.

content.introduction: The next Fed interest rate decision is scheduled for April 29, 2026. The Fed held rates steady at 3.5–3.75% in March. The Fed is likely to cut rates only if the labor market weakens significantly or if economic impacts from higher energy prices worsen.
body: # When is the next Fed interest rate decision? ## The next Federal Reserve (Fed) interest rate decision is due on Wednesday, April 29, 2026, at 18:00 GMT. The US Federal Reserve (Fed) announces its decisions regarding interest rates eight times a year during Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings., approximately every six weeks. ## What is the Fed interest rate? At its latest meeting on **March 18, 2026**, the FOMC **held interest rates unchanged**. ## What date is the next Federal Reserve interest rate meeting? **The next Federal Reserve meeting is scheduled for April 28–29, 2026.**‍. The federal funds rate target range is currently set at **3.50% to 3.75%**, where it was **left unchanged at the Federal Reserve’s latest meeting on March 18, 2026**, following a series of rate cuts in late 2025. ### What is the effect when the Federal Reserve increases interest rates? ## What time is the Fed rate decision? ### **Federal Reserve (Fed) interest rate**.

[Global Research](https://www.jpmorgan.com/insights/global-research). *   [Will interest rates go down in April?](https://www.jpmorgan.com/insights/global-research/economy/fed-rate-cuts#section-header#0). *   [What’s the outlook for rate cuts in 2026?](https://www.jpmorgan.com/insights/global-research/economy/fed-rate-cuts#section-header#1). *   [Will interest rates go down in April?](https://www.jpmorgan.com/insights/global-research/economy/fed-rate-cuts#section-header#0). *   [What’s the outlook for rate cuts in 2026?](https://www.jpmorgan.com/insights/global-research/economy/fed-rate-cuts#section-header#1). While the [Middle East conflict](https://www.jpmorgan.com/insights/global-research/current-events/iran-conflict-ripple-effects) has sent energy prices soaring, the Federal Reserve (Fed) opted to keep interest rates steady at 3.5–3.75% at its last meeting in March. Generally, the Fed is seen as more likely to cut rates only if the [labor market](https://www.jpmorgan.com/insights/global-research/outlook/labor-market-forecast-2026) weakens significantly or if the economic fallout from higher energy prices becomes more severe.
